  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The intro centers on a contribution claim ('first procedure') and an explicitly stated gap (poor calibration of CATE intervals); evaluating the thesis and gap statement is core to any plausible review.",
    "R1.2": "The argument chain — ML nuisance learners → slow convergence → miscoverage → conformal calibration fixes it — must be logically coherent; gaps or non-sequiturs in this chain would undermine the section regardless of review type.",
    "R1.3": "Specific quantitative claims are made (1.5 pp of nominal, 8–15 pt undercoverage, <30% width inflation) without in-text citation to tables or experiments; alignment between these numbers and any supporting evidence warrants scrutiny.",
    "R1.4": "The paper explicitly claims CalDML is the 'first' such procedure and frames a meaningful step beyond prior art; validating that framing against the cited and uncited literature is a natural review responsibility.",
    "R1.6": "Writing quality is a standard expectation for any section-level review; sentences are mostly clear but some are dense (e.g., the conformal exchangeability sentence) and warrant polish assessment.",
    "R1.7": "Six specific references are named with years; verifying year accuracy, correct attribution of results (e.g., 'root-n normality' to Chernozhukov et al.), and coverage gaps in the related-work paragraph is squarely in scope."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should this review prioritize writing clarity and narrative flow (making R1.6 and R1.5 load-bearing), or technical and scholarly accuracy — verifying the novelty claim, logical argument, quantitative assertions, and citation correctness (making R1.1, R1.2, R1.3, R1.4, R1.7 load-bearing)?"

rationale.R1.1: The intro centers on a contribution claim ('first procedure') and an explicitly stated gap (poor calibration of CATE intervals); evaluating the thesis and gap statement is core to any plausible review.
rationale.R1.2: The argument chain — ML nuisance learners → slow convergence → miscoverage → conformal calibration fixes it — must be logically coherent; gaps or non-sequiturs in this chain would undermine the section regardless of review type.
rationale.R1.3: Specific quantitative claims are made (1.5 pp of nominal, 8–15 pt undercoverage, <30% width inflation) without in-text citation to tables or experiments; alignment between these numbers and any supporting evidence warrants scrutiny.
rationale.R1.4: The paper explicitly claims CalDML is the 'first' such procedure and frames a meaningful step beyond prior art; validating that framing against the cited and uncited literature is a natural review responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: Writing quality is a standard expectation for any section-level review; sentences are mostly clear but some are dense (e.g., the conformal exchangeability sentence) and warrant polish assessment.
rationale.R1.7: Six specific references are named with years; verifying year accuracy, correct attribution of results (e.g., 'root-n normality' to Chernozhukov et al.), and coverage gaps in the related-work paragraph is squarely in scope.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review prioritize writing clarity and narrative flow (making R1.6 and R1.5 load-bearing), or technical and scholarly accuracy — verifying the novelty claim, logical argument, quantitative assertions, and citation correctness (making R1.1, R1.2, R1.3, R1.4, R1.7 load-bearing)?
